title: Exochorda racemosa
taxonomy: Exochorda racemosa is classified within the family Hydrangeaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as hydrangea or hydrangeoid plants. Exochorda racemosa is further classified within the genus Exochorda, which includes a few species of flowering plants. The genus Exochorda is a part of the order Cornales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Cornales is a part of the clade asterids, a large group of flowering plants.

intro: Exochorda racemosa, also known as the pearl exochorda or Chinese pear exochorda, is a flowering plant species native to China, characterized by its white flowers and edible fruit. The health benefits of Exochorda racemosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine. The bioactive constituents of Exochorda racemosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
